content.problemStatement:
An array AAA of size N(N≥2)N (N \ge 2)N(N≥2), containing non-negative integers, is called good if all of the following conditions hold:


A1=A2A_1 = A_2A1​=A2​, or A1=0A_1 = 0A1​=0
AN=AN−1A_N = A_{N - 1}AN​=AN−1​, or AN=0A_N = 0AN​=0
Ai=Ai−1+Ai+1A_i = A_{i - 1} + A_{i + 1}Ai​=Ai−1​+Ai+1​, or Ai=0A_i = 0Ai​=0 for all 1<i<N1 < i < N1<i<N.
Less formally, we need all non-zero AiA_iAi​ equal to the sum of it's adjacent elements.

You are given an array AAA of size NNN, consisting of non-negative integers.

In one operation, you can add 111 or subtract 111 from some element in AAA. It is not allowed to make the elements in AAA negative at any point.

Find the minimum operations to make the array AAA good. It can be proven that it is always possible.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN - the size of the array AAA.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum number of operations to make AAA good.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : We can reduce A2A_2A2​ by 555 to get [0,0][0, 0][0,0] which is good. We could also have increased A1A_1A1​ by 555 to get [5,5][5, 5][5,5] which is also good.
Test Case 2 : We can reduce A2A_2A2​ by 111 to get [3,3][3, 3][3,3] which is good.
